description: Shell company integration scheme — luxury goods purchased for cash, resold, and proceeds deposited to business account
parsed_text:
SUSPICIOUS ACTIVITY REPORT
SAR ID: SAR-2024-01877
Filing Institution: Metropolitan Trust Bank
Filing Date: 2024-08-19
Subject: Pinnacle Luxury Resale LLC   Tax ID: XX-XXXXXXX (on file)
Subject Address: 2240 Brickell Avenue, Suite 800, Miami, FL 33129
Account: XXXX4453
Activity Dates: 2024-02-01 to 2024-07-31
Suspicious Activity Type: MoneyLaundering
Amount Involved: USD 1,640,000.00   Transaction Count: 34
Law Enforcement Contacted: No
Branch: Miami Brickell Branch
Investigator: Carlos Reyes

Narrative: Metropolitan Trust Bank is filing this Suspicious Activity Report concerning integration-phase money laundering activity conducted through the business checking account of Pinnacle Luxury Resale LLC ('Pinnacle'), a Florida limited liability company engaged in secondary-market sales of high-end watches, handbags, and jewelry.

During the review period February 1, 2024 through July 31, 2024, the Pinnacle account received thirty-four (34) cash and check deposits totaling $1,640,000. Compliance staff initiated a review following a branch teller alert noting repeated large-denomination cash deposits accompanied by handwritten inventory receipts that referenced watch and handbag purchases at prices significantly below retail.

Bank records reflect the following pattern: Pinnacle made cash payments ranging from $8,000 to $45,000 to individual sellers for luxury goods, as documented on consignment receipts retained by the branch. The goods were subsequently listed and sold through the company's online marketplace and boutique storefront, with sale proceeds deposited as business revenue. The goods' provenance documentation was absent or incomplete in the majority of transactions reviewed.

A beneficial ownership review of Pinnacle revealed a sole member, Mr. Raul Fontaine (date of birth 06/22/1978), who holds no other verifiable employment or business income on file with the bank. Mr. Fontaine was unable to identify a consistent supplier base when interviewed by the bank's relationship manager in June 2024. Third-party open-source research identified two prior civil judgments against Mr. Fontaine for breach of contract in connection with separate business ventures.

The bank notes that cash purchases of luxury goods from private individuals with no documentation of provenance, combined with the deposit of resale proceeds into a business account, is consistent with the integration stage of a money laundering cycle. No prior SARs are on file for Pinnacle or Mr. Fontaine.

expected_json.narrative_summary: Pinnacle Luxury Resale LLC received $1,640,000 in deposits over six months derived from the resale of luxury goods purchased for cash from private individuals with no provenance documentation, consistent with integration-phase money laundering. The beneficial owner could not provide a coherent supplier base, and prior civil judgments were identified through open-source research.
